name: Carnegie Mellon University
meets_full_need_notes: CMU does not guarantee to meet 100% of demonstrated financial need. Aid is substantial but may not fully cover calculated need for all students. Institutional aid is competitive.
no_loan_notes: CMU includes loans in financial aid packages. No institution-wide no-loan policy, though some programs may vary.

merit_thresholds.notes: CMU offers merit scholarships including the Presidential Scholarship, Dean's Scholarship, and college-specific awards. Scholarships are awarded at admission; no separate application required for most. Amounts vary by college (SCS, CIT, Tepper, etc.).

ed_aid_implications: CMU's ED is binding but students may appeal the aid package. Merit scholarships are awarded the same way regardless of admission round. Need-based aid is need-blind for domestic students in ED.
special_programs: College-specific merit awards (SCS, Tepper, CIT, etc.)
appeal_policy: CMU has a formal appeal process. Students submit appeals to the Office of Undergraduate Financial Aid with documentation of changed circumstances.
